trialDesign.fullName: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease
trialDesign.overview: ## BACKGROUND Patients with chronic kidney disease have a high risk of adverse kidney and cardiovascular outcomes. The effect of dapagliflozin in patients with chronic kidney disease, with or without type 2 diabetes, is not known.
trialDesign.design: We randomly assigned 4304 participants with an estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) of 25 to 75 ml per minute per 1.73 m of body-surface area and a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio (with albumin measured in milligrams and creatinine measured in grams) of 200 to 5000 to receive dapagliflozin
trialDesign.primaryEndpoint: primary outcome event occurred in 197 of 2152 participants (9.

headings.background: Patients with chronic kidney disease have a high risk of adverse kidney and cardiovascular outcomes. The effect of dapagliflozin in patients with chronic kidney disease, with or without type 2 diabetes, is not known.
headings.objective: Patients with chronic kidney disease have a high risk of adverse kidney and cardiovascular outcomes. The effect of dapagliflozin in patients with chronic kidney disease, with or without type 2 diabetes, is not known.
headings.design: We randomly assigned 4304 participants with an estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) of 25 to 75 ml per minute per 1.73 m of body-surface area and a urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio (with albumin measured in milligrams and creatinine measured in grams) of 200 to 5000 to receive dapagliflozin

outcomes.label: Primary outcome (extracted)
outcomes.definition: primary outcome event occurred in 197 of 2152 participants (9.
outcomes.interpretation: Among patients with chronic kidney disease, regardless of the presence or absence of diabetes, the risk of a composite of a sustained decline in the estimated GFR of at least 50%, end-stage kidney disease, or death from renal or cardiovascular causes was significantly lower with dapagliflozin than w

trialSummary.conclusion: Among patients with chronic kidney disease, regardless of the presence or absence of diabetes, the risk of a composite of a sustained decline in the estimated GFR of at least 50%, end-stage kidney disease, or death from renal or cardiovascular causes was significantly lower with dapagliflozin than with placebo. (Funded by AstraZeneca; DAPA-CKD ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT03036150.) WORLDWIDE ESTIMATES INDICATE that nearly 700 million persons have chronic kidney disease. Chronic kidney disease is an important contributor to illness and is associated with a diminished quality of life and a red

items.question: Why is the following clinically relevant in DAPA-CKD? Context: blocks to ensure an approximate 1:1 ratio of the two regimens. Randomization was stratified according to the diagnosis o…
items.answer: blocks to ensure an approximate 1:1 ratio of the two regimens. Randomization was stratified according to the diagnosis of type 2 diabetes (yes or no) and the urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio ( or >1000). Investigators used an interactive voice-response or Web-response system to determine trial-group assignments. Randomization was monitored to ensure that a minimum of 30% of the participants were recruited to either the population with type 2 diabetes or the population without diabetes. Recruitment of participants with an estimated GFR of 60 to 75 ml per minute per 1.73 m was halted on November 27, 2017, to ensure that no more than 10% of the trial participants had stage 2 chronic kidney disease. Participants and all trial personnel (except the members of the independent data monitoring committee) were unaware of the trial-group assignments. Dapagliflozin and placebo were packaged identically, with uniform tablet appearance, labeling, and administration schedules

items.question: How does the process described in reference 3 relate to DAPA-CKD?
items.answer: The kidney-protective effects of SGLT2 inhibitors have previously been shown in patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease in the CREDENCE trial. In that trial, as in most previous cardiovascular outcome trials of SGLT2 inhibitors, the lower estimated GFR cutoff for inclusion was 30 ml per minute per 1.73 m . In contrast to the CREDENCE trial, the present trial examined the effects of an SGLT2 inhibitor in patients with chronic kidney disease of whom 32.5% did not have type 2 diabetes and 14.5% had an estimated GFR below 30 ml per minute per 1.73 m . Our trial confirms that the kidney-protective effects of SGLT2 inhibitors extend to the broader population of persons with chronic kidney disease without type 2 diabetes, for whom ACE inhibitors are the only pharmacologic treatments that have been shown to prevent kidney failure.

items.question: Why is the following clinically relevant in DAPA-CKD? Context: Sodium–glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors decrease glycated hemoglobin levels and have shown favorable effects o…
items.answer: Sodium–glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors decrease glycated hemoglobin levels and have shown favorable effects on kidney and cardiovascular outcomes in large clinical trials involving patients with type 2 diabetes. The CREDENCE (Canagliflozin and Renal Events in Diabetes with Established Nephropathy Clinical Evaluation) trial showed that long-term administration of canagliflozin conferred renal and cardiovascular protection in patients with type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease. Although the underlying mechanisms are not completely understood, the benefits of SGLT2 inhibitors appear to be independent of their blood glucose-lowering effects and may be mediated by natriuresis and glucose-induced osmotic diuresis, leading to a reduction in intraglomerular pressure. This favorable hemodynamic effect may also preserve kidney function in persons with kidney diseases due to causes other than type 2 diabetes. We designed the Dapagliflozin and Prevention of Adverse Outcomes in Chronic Kidney Disease (DAPA-CKD) trial to assess the long-term efficacy and safety of the SGLT2 inhibitor dapagliflozin in patients with chronic kidney disease, with or without type 2 diabetes
items.reference: Ref 7: "Sodium–glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors decrease glycated hemoglobin levels and have shown favorable effects on kidney and cardiovascular outcomes in ..." — Wanner C, Inzucchi SE, Lachin JM, et al. items.question: Why is the following clinically relevant in DAPA-CKD? Context: Our trial was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter clinical trial; details regarding the trial de…
items.answer: Our trial was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter clinical trial; details regarding the trial design and baseline characteristics of the participants have been published previously. The trial was sponsored by AstraZeneca and conducted at 386 sites in 21 countries from February 2, 2017, through June 12, 2020. An executive committee consisting of nine academic members and two nonvoting employees of the sponsor was responsible for the design and oversight of the trial and the reporting of the results. The trial protocol (available with the full text of this article at NEJM.org) was approved by a central or local ethics committee at each trial site. All the participants provided written informed consent before any trial-specific procedure commenced. The safety of the participants was overseen by an independent data monitoring committee. The analyses that were conducted by the sponsor were replicated by an independent academic group at the University Medical Center Groningen. The first draft of the manuscript was written by the first and last authors and was revised by the coauthors. The decision to submit the manuscript for publication was made jointly by all the authors. The first and last authors vouch for the completeness and accuracy of the data and for the fidelity of the trial to the protocol

items.question: Which finding is supported by reference 17 in DAPA-CKD?
options: Placebo response only
Not reported in this trial
Opposite direction of effect
See reference text
items.explanation: ops. Global estimates indicate that 1.2 million deaths were attributable to chronic kidney disease in 2017. The findings from the present trial confirm the high mortality among patients with impaired kidney function. The markedly lower mortality in the dapagliflozin group than in the placebo group supports the use of dapagliflozin as an addition to the therapeutic management of chronic kidney disease.

items.question: How does the process described in reference 18 relate to DAPA-CKD?
items.answer: Participants in our trial also had a high risk of hospitalization for heart failure or death from cardiovascular causes. The lower risk of hospitalization for heart failure or death from cardiovascular causes in the dapagliflozin group than in the placebo group is consistent with the results of two previous trials of dapagliflozin, the DECLARE-TIMI 58 (Dapagliflozin Effect on Cardiovascular Events-Thrombolysis in Myocardial Infarction 58) and DAPA-HF (Dapagliflozin and Prevention of Adverse Outcomes in Heart Failure) trials. Clinical trials of other SGLT2 inhibitors have shown similar results.

items.question: How does the process described in reference 20 relate to DAPA-CKD?
items.answer: Our trial has some limitations. First, the trial was stopped on the basis of a recommendation from the independent data monitoring committee. This may have reduced the power of some secondary outcomes. However, the strong internal and external validity of the treatment effect suggests that this limitation is unlikely to have had a major influence on the findings. As in other trials of SGLT2 inhibitors, there was an initial dip in the estimated GFR, followed by a stabilization of kidney-function decline. This dip in the estimated GFR reflects favorable hemodynamic changes in the glomerulus. We did not collect estimated GFR values after the completion of the trial and are unable to ascertain whether the initial dip in the estimated GFR is reversible after the discontinuation of dapagliflozin, as observed in other dapagliflozin studies.
items.reference: Ref 20: "Our trial has some limitations. First, the trial was stopped on the basis of a recommendation from the independent data monitoring committee. items.stem: What is the primary clinical relevance of using stratified randomization and blinding in the DAPA-CKD trial?
options: A) To reduce the risk of adverse events in participants
B) To ensure equal distribution of dapagliflozin and placebo across all subgroups
C) To improve drug efficacy by increasing participant compliance
D) To minimize selection bias and ensure comparability between treatment groups
items.explanation: Stratified randomization (by diabetes status and albumin-to-creatinine ratio) and blinding (via identical packaging) are critical to minimize selection bias and ensure that treatment groups are comparable, allowing for valid assessment of the intervention's effects.
items.distillation_trace:
Okay, let's tackle this query. The user wants me to convert the given Q&A into an objective exam question in JSON format, specifically for Harrison's Principles of Internal Medicine. The original question is about the DAPA-CKD trial, focusing on randomization methods and their clinical relevance.

First, I need to determine the best question type. The original answer discusses randomization techniques, stratification factors, and blinding. Since this involves specific methods and their purposes, an MCQ seems appropriate. True/false might be too simplistic, and assertion-reason could work but MCQ allows for more detailed options.

Next, the stem should ask why a specific aspect of the trial is clinically relevant. The answer mentions stratification by diabetes status and albumin-to-creatinine ratio, using a randomization system, and blinding. The key point here is ensuring balanced groups and reducing bias, which are crucial for trial validity.

Now, creating the options. The correct answer should be about minimizing bias and ensuring balanced groups. Distractors need to be plausible but incorrect. items.stem: How does the DAPA-CKD trial differ from the CREDENCE trial in terms of patient inclusion criteria for evaluating SGLT2 inhibitors?
options: A) DAPA-CKD excluded patients with estimated GFR <30 ml/min/1.73 m², unlike CREDENCE
B) CREDENCE included a higher proportion of patients without type 2 diabetes compared to DAPA-CKD
C) DAPA-CKD included patients with chronic kidney disease regardless of diabetes status and with estimated GFR as low as <30 ml/min/1.73 m²
D) Both trials required type 2 diabetes for enrollment but differed in cardiovascular outcomes measured
items.explanation: DAPA-CKD expanded eligibility to include patients without type 2 diabetes and with lower eGFR thresholds compared to CREDENCE, which primarily studied patients with type 2 diabetes and eGFR ≥30 ml/min/1.73 m².

items.explanation: The DAPA-CKD trial's cardiovascular benefits (reduced heart failure hospitalizations and cardiovascular death) are consistent with DAPA-HF and DECLARE-TIMI 58, which also showed similar outcomes with dapagliflozin.

items.stem: Which trials support the cardiovascular benefits of dapagliflozin observed in the DAPA-CKD study?

B) DAPA-HF and DECLARE-TIMI 58
C) LEADER and SUSTAIN-6
D) CREDENCE and FIDELIO-DKD
items.explanation: DAPA-HF and DECLARE-TIMI 58 demonstrated reduced hospitalization for heart failure and cardiovascular death with dapagliflozin, consistent with DAPA-CKD findings.

items.stem: What is the primary limitation of the DAPA-CKD trial related to its early termination by the data monitoring committee?
options: A) The trial's internal validity was compromised due to incomplete follow-up
B) The initial dip in eGFR observed with dapagliflozin was irreversible
C) The early termination reduced statistical power for secondary outcomes but not the primary efficacy endpoint
D) Hemodynamic changes in the glomerulus led to permanent kidney function decline
items.explanation: The trial was stopped early based on the data monitoring committee's recommendation, which may have reduced power for secondary outcomes but not the primary findings, as the treatment effect showed strong internal/external validity.

items.stem: What is the primary clinical relevance of the DAPA-CKD trial regarding SGLT2 inhibitors?
options: A) They reduce glycated hemoglobin levels exclusively through glucose-lowering effects
B) They improve renal outcomes solely in patients with type 2 diabetes
C) Their benefits are independent of glycemic control and involve hemodynamic mechanisms
D) They prevent cardiovascular events only in patients with advanced chronic kidney disease
items.explanation: SGLT2 inhibitors confer renal and cardiovascular benefits through mechanisms independent of glycemic control, such as natriuresis and reduced intraglomerular pressure, as demonstrated in DAPA-CKD.
